title: Uniface 10: Changes in ProcScript
breadcrumbs: What's New in Uniface 10 Compared to Uniface 9
text: Uniface 10: Changes in ProcScript New ProcScript instructions have been added and existing ProcScript has been enhanced compared with Uniface 9. New ProcScript Commands Table 1. New ProcScript Commands ProcScript Description Release rethrow Rethrow an exception that was caught in a catch block. 10.4 .02 finally Declares ProcScript inside a try-endtry block that must be executed, regardless of whether an exception occurs or not. 10.4 .02 throw , throw/list Throw a custom exception. 10.4 .02 throws Declares that any runtime error ( $procerror ) that occurs in the module will be regarded as an exception. 10.4 .02 try Block declaration that specifies the code that can throw an exception. 10.4 .02 endtry Defines the end of the try block. 10.4 .02 catch Specifies code within the try block that will handle any resulting exceptions. 10.4 .02 undeclare Force the specified ProcScript module to be ignored during compilation. 10.2.03 $instancelayout Every server page component has a layout property which is set declaratively using the Layout Editor, and read by webgen and weblayout when creating or refreshing a component instance. You can use the $instancelayout function to read the current layout or set a new layout dynamically at runtime. 10.1.01 $ude ("archive") Copy compiled runtime objects from the Resource Output ($RSO) path to a Uniface archive (UAR) file. 10.3.03 $ude ("lookup") Look up existing runtime objects, symbol tables, or script listings. 10.3.03 $webinfo("Locale") Set the client-side locale in a DSP application. The value is used by the DSP client for locale-based formatting. 10.3.03 New Module Type Table 2. New Module Type Module name Description Release function Uniface now supports a new ProcScript module type function . The function module can help you in keeping code more self-contained. See the related documentation for details: function...end 10.4.02.16 Changes in ProcScript Commands Table 3. Changes in ProcScript Commands ProcScript Description Release Variables When defining component variables, it is possible to specify a display format. Specifying a DTD or schema for an xmlstream parameter is not supported for component variables and is deprecated for other variable declarations. 10.2.01 $procerrorcontext The command has been enhanced to return more information about the error context, including the command where the error was encountered, the trigger return value (in some circumstances), and the call stack. 10.4 .02 $ude ("export") Definitions are now exported in order of primary key. The command has been extended to export projects and snippet libraries. Constants and field templates can no longer be specified as separate objects for export because constants are automatically exported when the parent library is exported, and field templates are exported as part of an entity palette. 10.2.01 $ude ("import") Additional checks have been added to the import process to prevent data being imported from an incompatible database. 10.1.01 $ude("import") can no longer be used to import data created by the data conversion (copy) facilities such as /cpy and $ude("copy") . 10.2.01 $widgetoperation The Output Box now supports widget operations that enable you to add lines to the data stream, and copy the current contents of the widget to a variable. 10.2.02 New and Changed Precompiler Directives and Predefined Constants Table 4. New and Changed Precompiler Directives and Predefined Constants ProcScript Description Release #startdefine #enddefine The #startdefine and #enddefine directives enable you to explicitly define the scope of constant declarations ( #define directives). By default, constants have container scope, so they are applicable for all triggers, operations, functions, and entries in a Script container. You can use these directives to narrow the scope to a specific script module, or even to a group of modules. New in 10.2.01 <$triggerAbbr> The <$triggerAbbr> constant returns the abbreviation of the code container. This is of limited use for debugging, so it is now possible to redefine the value using #define . By default, Uniface adds <$triggerAbbr> definitions when migrating ProcScript to Uniface 10. Changed in 10.2.01 Deprecated ProcScript Deprecated ProcScript commands will eventually not be supported and removed from the product. It is recommended that you update your code to use the commands that supersede the deprecated commands. Table 5. Deprecated ProcScript Commands Deprecated Command Superseded by run activate $formdb $instancedb $formdbmod $instancedbmod $formmod $instancemod $formname $instancename and $componentname Unsupported ProcScript The following ProcScript commands were deprecated in Uniface 9, and are not supported in Uniface 10. They result in a warning message when compiled and must be replaced by the current ProcScript command. They will no longer work in the next major release (Uniface 11).
